html:
Johns Manville Systems for Odessa Low-Slope Roofs

Johns Manville built its reputation on insulation and asphalt-based roofing products long before single-ply membrane became the industry standard, and that background still shows in the breadth of its current product catalog. For Odessa's mix of older asphalt-roofed buildings and newer metal building retrofits, that range matters more than it would in a market with more uniform building stock.

Company Background Relevant to Roofing Decisions

Johns Manville is a subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway, headquartered in Denver, Colorado, and manufactures both roofing membrane and the insulation products used beneath it across residential, commercial, and industrial applications. That combined insulation and membrane manufacturing base is relevant on projects where insulation performance and membrane compatibility are being specified together rather than sourced from separate suppliers, which simplifies the warranty picture on the finished assembly.

The company's roofing division covers TPO single-ply membrane, modified bitumen systems, and built-up roofing components, positioning it as one of the few manufacturers with a genuinely full range spanning both legacy asphalt-based systems and current single-ply technology.

That range matters when a bid comes back recommending a system an owner has never heard of for their specific building type. A manufacturer that still actively develops and supports older asphalt-based technology, rather than treating it as a discontinued legacy product, gives owners of older buildings a real choice rather than being pushed toward single-ply purely because that is what a given manufacturer's catalog defaults to.

Modified Bitumen and Built-Up Roofing Relevance

Johns Manville's asphalt-based product lines are directly relevant to the older commercial buildings in downtown Odessa and the smaller surrounding towns still carrying built-up or modified bitumen roofs. Having a manufacturer that continues to actively support and manufacture these older system types, rather than having phased them out entirely in favor of single-ply, matters when an existing built-up roof needs a compatible recover rather than a full tear-off to bare deck.

SBS and APP modified bitumen options within the Johns Manville line give a recover project flexibility on application method, whether torch, hot-mopped, cold-adhesive, or self-adhered, depending on the building's occupancy and fire-safety requirements.

APP modified bitumen in particular tends to hold up well under sustained direct sun, which is a relevant consideration on the flat-roofed older commercial buildings around downtown Odessa that get long, unshaded exposure through West Texas summers with no adjacent structures to break up the sun load.

TPO Line for Newer Construction and Metal Building Retrofits

Johns Manville's TPO membrane covers standard thickness ranges relevant to the 60 mil and 80 mil decisions common on Odessa's warehouse and fabrication shop reroofs, along with fleece-backed reinforced options for higher-traffic applications. As with other manufacturers' TPO lines, thickness and reinforcement selection depend on the specific building's hail exposure, foot traffic, and rooftop equipment load rather than a single default specification.

  • TPO membrane in standard and fleece-backed thickness options
  • SBS and APP modified bitumen systems in multiple application methods
  • Built-up roofing components for legacy asphalt system recovers
  • Insulation products manufactured to work within the same roof assembly
  • Product support spanning both older asphalt-based and current single-ply technology

Insulation Performance in a Climate With Wide Temperature Swings

West Texas sees a wide daily and seasonal temperature range, hot summer days followed by cool desert nights, and insulation performance affects both energy cost and membrane longevity through thermal cycling stress. Because Johns Manville manufactures insulation and membrane together, insulation board thickness and type can be specified as part of the same system decision as the membrane itself, rather than as an afterthought sourced separately after the membrane spec is already locked in.

This matters most on metal building retrofits where the existing structure has little or no insulation, and adding adequate insulation as part of the reroof project meaningfully changes both comfort inside the building and stress on the new membrane over its service life.

Warranty Considerations Across the Product Range

Warranty terms vary across Johns Manville's TPO, modified bitumen, and built-up product lines, generally following the same industry pattern of material-only versus fuller system coverage tied to installation meeting current manufacturer program requirements. Because the company's product range spans older and newer technology, we confirm current program terms for the specific system being installed rather than assuming warranty structure is identical across every product in the catalog.

Questions on Johns Manville Roofing Systems

Does Johns Manville still manufacture built-up roofing components?

Yes, the company continues to manufacture asphalt-based systems including modified bitumen and built-up roofing components alongside its single-ply TPO line.

Is Johns Manville TPO comparable to other major manufacturers' TPO?

It covers similar thickness and reinforcement ranges to other major TPO manufacturers, with selection depending on the specific building's needs rather than a categorical difference between brands.

Can Johns Manville insulation be specified separately from its membrane?

Yes, though specifying both from the same manufacturer as part of one system can simplify warranty and compatibility considerations on the finished roof assembly.

Does an older built-up roof need to be torn off, or can it be recovered with Johns Manville products?

Recover is often possible depending on the existing roof's condition and code requirements. We evaluate the existing deck and insulation before recommending recover versus tear-off.

What warranty coverage is typical on a Johns Manville commercial roof?

Coverage ranges from material-only to fuller system warranties depending on the product line and installation requirements met, and we review the applicable terms before any contract is signed.
